entities.description: A curated Electronic Artefacts collection about durable AI and systems architecture.

entities.title: Voice Capture Studio Collection
entities.subtitle: Voice Workflow Collection
entities.summary: The Voice Capture Studio Collection connects the five-mode product, source repository and knowledge needed to understand signal-grounded UX, local audio pipelines, deterministic evidence and open voice datasets.
entities.description: A curated collection around Voice Capture Studio as a browser-native voice instrument and research surface.

entities.title: AI Agent
entities.summary: AI agents combine models, tools, memory, environmental feedback, stopping conditions, permissions and evaluation in an iterative action loop.
entities.description: A canonical definition distinguishing AI agents from fixed AI workflows and conventional automation.
entities.definition: An AI agent is a software system in which a model can select actions, use tools, observe results and adapt its next steps toward an objective within defined boundaries.

entities.title: Algorithmic Composition
entities.summary: Algorithmic composition connects music theory, computation, cybernetics, generative systems and electronic music by treating composition as the design of procedures as well as sounds.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for rule-based and computational approaches to musical composition.
entities.definition: Algorithmic composition is the use of rules, procedures, probability, computation or formal systems to generate or organize musical material.

entities.title: Augmented Intelligence
entities.summary: Augmented intelligence reframes computation as a partner in human work, linking HCI, AI, knowledge systems, creative tools and Electronic Artefacts runtime research.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for human-centered computational augmentation.
entities.definition: Augmented intelligence is the design of tools, interfaces and systems that expand human perception, reasoning, memory and creative capability.

entities.title: Autonomous System
entities.summary: Autonomous systems combine sensing, models, planning, execution, feedback, constraints, governance and human intervention paths.
entities.description: A canonical definition connecting AI agents, robotics, runtime systems and responsible control.
entities.definition: An autonomous system can perceive relevant state, select actions and adapt behavior toward objectives with limited direct human control during operation.

entities.title: Browser Software
entities.summary: Browser software treats the web browser as an application runtime, not only as a document viewer or marketing surface.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for applications built directly on browser capabilities.
entities.definition: Browser software is application software delivered through web standards while relying on the browser runtime for interface, storage, permissions, media and distribution behavior.

entities.title: Contextual Execution
entities.summary: Contextual execution describes runtime behavior where an operation is evaluated through entity identity, graph relations, permissions, temporal state and intended scope.
entities.description: A canonical Electronic Artefacts concept connecting graph runtime, Runtime Theory, VASTE and identity-aware systems.
entities.definition: Contextual execution is the practice of making identity, state, permissions, relationships and operating context active constraints on what a system can do.

entities.title: Creative Coding
entities.summary: Creative coding connects software, visual culture, sound, interaction, generative systems, education and experimental publishing.
entities.description: A canonical definition of creative coding for Electronic Artefacts knowledge articles, projects and programs.
entities.definition: Creative coding is the use of programming as an artistic, design or exploratory medium rather than only as an engineering implementation tool.

entities.title: Cybernetic Feedback
entities.summary: Cybernetic feedback connects control, communication, systems theory, generative art, machine listening, adaptive interfaces and runtime governance.
entities.description: A canonical definition of cybernetic feedback for Electronic Artefacts research, creative systems and historical technology context.
entities.definition: Cybernetic feedback is the circulation of information about a system's state back into the system so that behavior can be regulated, adapted or transformed.

entities.title: Digital Preservation
entities.summary: Digital preservation connects file formats, metadata, provenance, storage, rights, migration and interpretation into a long-term care practice for digital culture.
entities.description: A canonical definition of digital preservation for Electronic Artefacts archives, Palimpsests, Vestiges and knowledge infrastructure.
entities.definition: Digital preservation is the long-term work of keeping digital objects understandable, authentic, usable and accessible despite changes in formats, software, hardware and context.

entities.title: Domain Operating System
entities.summary: A domain operating system turns repeated domain rules into reusable infrastructure without claiming to replace a general-purpose computer operating system.
entities.description: An Electronic Artefacts concept for business operating systems, runtime engines and shared domain platforms.
entities.definition: A domain operating system is a shared runtime and information layer that coordinates identities, entities, permissions, workflows, events and interfaces for a specific field of activity.

entities.title: Entity Identity
entities.summary: Entity identity gives knowledge systems a way to cite things without confusing names, pages, files, versions and changing descriptions.
entities.description: A canonical definition of entity identity for graph publishing, semantic URLs, archives and Electronic Artefacts records.
entities.definition: Entity identity is the durable naming of a person, concept, project, program, publication or artefact so that references remain stable as descriptions evolve.

entities.title: Event-Driven Architecture
entities.summary: Event-driven architecture connects event producers, brokers or logs, consumers, schemas, delivery semantics, projections and observability.
entities.description: A canonical definition of event-driven architecture for runtimes, workflows and distributed systems.
entities.definition: Event-driven architecture is a system design approach in which components publish, transport and react to records of state change rather than depending only on direct synchronous calls.

entities.title: Generative AI
entities.summary: Generative AI connects machine learning, creative workflows, latent spaces, risk management, provenance, authorship and cultural production.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for generative AI in creative and knowledge systems.
entities.definition: Generative AI refers to machine-learning systems that produce or transform media such as text, image, audio, video, code or structured data from learned patterns.

entities.title: Generative System
entities.summary: Generative systems connect creative coding, cybernetics, algorithmic composition, simulation, design tools and AI by shifting authorship toward rules, feedback and selection.
entities.description: A canonical definition of generative system for creative coding, sound design, design automation and Electronic Artefacts projects.
entities.definition: A generative system is a rule-based, procedural or computational arrangement that produces variable outputs from defined constraints, materials and processes.

entities.title: Graph Modeling
entities.summary: Graph modeling makes many-to-many relationships, paths, provenance and evolving context explicit where folders and rigid trees obscure them.
entities.description: A canonical definition of graph modeling for knowledge, runtime and cultural systems.
entities.definition: Graph modeling represents a domain through addressable nodes, typed relationships and properties chosen to answer specific questions and support specific operations.

entities.title: Graph Runtime
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts uses graph runtime to describe systems where entities, relationships, identity, context and events form an executable structure rather than a passive data model.
entities.description: A canonical definition of graph runtime, its scope, applications and relationship to VASTE, Runtime Theory and Vestiges.
entities.definition: A graph runtime is an execution environment in which addressable entities and typed relationships participate directly in contextual computation.

entities.title: Human Computer Interaction
entities.summary: HCI connects interface design, accessibility, creative tools, augmented intelligence, software ergonomics and the social consequences of computation.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for HCI as a design and research discipline.
entities.definition: Human computer interaction is the study and design of relationships between people, computational systems, interfaces, tasks, environments and cultures of use.

entities.title: Hypertext
entities.summary: Hypertext is a foundational concept for the web, digital archives, knowledge graphs, digital gardens, public notes and Electronic Artefacts knowledge publishing.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for linked textual and media systems.
entities.definition: Hypertext is a system of non-linear text and media connected by links, allowing readers and machines to traverse knowledge through associations.

entities.title: Internet Culture
entities.summary: Internet culture connects network protocols, web publishing, platform communities, memes, archives, open source, digital folklore and public knowledge systems.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for networked cultural production and memory.
entities.definition: Internet culture is the set of social forms, aesthetics, protocols, communities, memes, archives and practices that emerge through networked communication.

entities.title: Knowledge Graph
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts uses knowledge graph to describe public knowledge systems where concepts, projects, programs, publications, sources and artefacts keep stable identities and typed relations.
entities.description: A canonical definition of knowledge graph for cultural, technical and research publishing.
entities.definition: A knowledge graph is a structured network of entities, identifiers and typed relationships that makes knowledge navigable, queryable and reusable across contexts.

entities.title: Large Language Model
entities.summary: Large language models combine tokenization, embeddings, transformer layers, attention, training objectives and probabilistic decoding to generate or transform language.
entities.description: A canonical definition of large language models, their operating principles, capabilities and limitations.
entities.definition: A large language model is a parameterized machine-learning model trained to predict and generate sequences of tokens from statistical patterns learned across large text or multimodal corpora.

entities.title: Linked Data
entities.summary: Linked Data gives public knowledge systems a way to make entities, pages, references and datasets interoperable across sites and software systems.
entities.description: A canonical definition of Linked Data as it applies to Electronic Artefacts, JSON-LD, RDF, SEO and public knowledge pages.
entities.definition: Linked Data is a web publishing approach that identifies things with stable URIs and connects them through machine-readable statements.

entities.title: Machine Learning Workflows
entities.summary: Machine learning workflows depend on source quality, dataset structure, documentation, evaluation and governance as much as on model architecture.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for downstream machine-learning preparation and evaluation paths.
entities.definition: Machine learning workflows are the ordered practices that move from source material and metadata through validation, preparation, training, evaluation and deployment decisions.

entities.title: Metadata
entities.summary: Metadata is the descriptive, administrative, structural and preservation layer that makes archives, catalogues, digital objects and knowledge graphs usable over time.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for metadata in cultural and technical systems.
entities.definition: Metadata is structured information about a resource, such as title, creator, date, format, source, rights, subject, relation and preservation context.

entities.title: Microservice Architecture
entities.summary: Microservices can improve independent evolution and scaling but add network, data, deployment, observability and organizational complexity.
entities.description: A canonical definition comparing microservices with modular monoliths and service-oriented systems.
entities.definition: Microservice architecture structures a software system as independently deployable services aligned with bounded capabilities and communicating through explicit contracts.

entities.title: Motion Design
entities.summary: Motion design connects visual culture, interface semantics, typography, animation, sound, choreography and web interaction.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for motion as a design and interface discipline.
entities.definition: Motion design is the use of time, movement, transition, rhythm and sequencing to structure visual communication and interface behavior.

entities.title: Multimodal AI
entities.summary: Multimodal AI connects representation learning, modality encoders, shared embedding spaces, generation, alignment and cross-modal interfaces.
entities.description: A canonical definition of multimodal AI for creative systems, archives and machine perception.
entities.definition: Multimodal AI describes systems that learn from, align, transform or generate more than one modality such as text, image, audio, video, sensor data or structured records.

entities.title: Ontology
entities.summary: Ontologies make domain meaning explicit and interoperable, while taxonomies organize concepts primarily through classification and hierarchy.
entities.description: A canonical definition distinguishing ontology, taxonomy, thesaurus and folksonomy.
entities.definition: In knowledge engineering, an ontology is an explicit specification of the kinds of entities, properties, relationships and constraints used to represent a domain.

entities.title: Open Source
entities.summary: Open source is both a licensing model and a cultural infrastructure for software, creative tools, public knowledge, maintainership and technical education.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for open source software and cultural infrastructure.
entities.definition: Open source is a software and cultural practice in which source code is made available under licenses that allow use, study, modification and redistribution.

entities.title: Open-Weight Model
entities.summary: Open-weight models enable local inference and adaptation but must be distinguished from fully open-source AI systems.
entities.description: A canonical definition for local AI, model licensing and digital independence.
entities.definition: An open-weight model is a machine-learning model whose learned parameters are distributed for reuse under stated terms, without necessarily providing the training data, training code or freedoms required by open-source definitions.

entities.title: Personal Knowledge System
entities.summary: Personal knowledge systems connect digital gardens, wikis, note archives, research methods, backlinks and long-term intellectual memory.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for durable note and knowledge practices.
entities.definition: A personal knowledge system is a practice and tool environment for capturing, linking, revising and retrieving notes, sources, ideas and decisions over time.

entities.title: Procedural Graphics
entities.summary: Procedural graphics connect shaders, generative systems, motion design, creative coding, visual culture and real-time web interfaces.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for rule-based visual generation.
entities.definition: Procedural graphics are images or visual systems generated from rules, algorithms, parameters, simulation or code rather than only manually drawn assets.

entities.title: Provenance
entities.summary: Provenance helps readers assess reliability, rights, authorship, lineage, context and trust across research, archive, software and cultural records.
entities.description: A canonical definition of provenance for Electronic Artefacts publications, artefacts, archives and graph relations.
entities.definition: Provenance is information about the people, activities, sources, transformations and decisions involved in producing a thing or piece of data.

entities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
entities.summary: RAG connects search, embeddings, document segmentation, metadata, provenance, context assembly, language models and answer evaluation.
entities.description: A canonical definition of retrieval-augmented generation for knowledge systems and source-grounded AI.
entities.definition: Retrieval-augmented generation is an architecture that retrieves external information at query time and supplies selected evidence to a generative model before or during response production.

entities.title: Signal Archaeology
entities.summary: Signal archaeology gives Electronic Artefacts a way to connect audio analysis, media residue, archival provenance, memory and cultural interpretation.
entities.description: A canonical Electronic Artefacts concept linking ORETH, Palimpsests, digital preservation, provenance and machine listening.
entities.definition: Signal archaeology is the interpretation of technical, sonic, visual or archival traces as evidence of hidden processes, transformations and histories.

entities.title: Situated Actors and Topological Governance
entities.summary: How VASTE separates identity, credentials, capabilities, policy and effective authority while placing the observer inside the graph.
entities.description: A public account of Actor identity, graph-derived governance, local authority and portable declarations in VASTE.
entities.definition: In VASTE, an Actor is an extension-owned graph identity whose effective observation and action are resolved inside a specific System, Environment, topology and policy context.

entities.title: Speech Datasets
entities.summary: Speech datasets turn individual voice recordings into reviewable, documented and reusable collections without erasing the conditions of capture.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for structured speech data and voice-capture metadata.
entities.definition: Speech datasets are structured collections of recorded speech, transcripts, speaker context and quality metadata prepared for analysis, preservation or downstream machine-learning workflows.

entities.title: Speech Recording
entities.summary: Speech recording connects audio quality, speaker context, prompts, transcripts, metadata and review workflows into a durable voice record.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for professional speech capture and review workflows.
entities.definition: Speech recording is the directed capture of spoken language with enough technical, contextual and consent metadata to support review, reuse and preservation.

entities.title: Systems Thinking
entities.summary: Systems thinking helps creators and technologists understand why local interventions can produce indirect, delayed or counterintuitive effects.
entities.description: A canonical definition of systems thinking for creative practice, software architecture and organizational design.
entities.definition: Systems thinking is an approach that studies elements through their relationships, feedback, boundaries, delays, stocks, flows and patterns of behavior over time.

entities.title: Typography
entities.summary: Typography connects design, reading systems, accessibility, visual culture, web interfaces and long-form knowledge publishing.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for typography in digital interfaces and cultural publishing.
entities.definition: Typography is the design and arrangement of written language through typefaces, spacing, hierarchy, rhythm, scale and reading conditions.

entities.title: VAB and Genesis
entities.summary: What VAB currently implements, how explicit assent seals a boot plan, and what remains before a complete Electronic Artefacts Genesis handover.
entities.description: A maturity-aware guide to VASTE Assisted Boot, Founding Actor admission, composition and Genesis.
entities.definition: VAB is VASTE’s Assisted Boot orchestration path for understanding an initial intent, composing a sealed plan, admitting a Founding Actor and materializing a governed starting graph.

entities.title: .vast Portable Graph Format
entities.summary: The implemented .vast export/import model, integrity guarantees, boundary references, tested round-trip and unresolved trust and migration limits.
entities.description: A factual public guide to the experimental VASTE portable graph package format.
entities.definition: A .vast file is an experimental `vast/1` sealed JSON package carrying a System-relative whole-System or subtree closure, its manifest and integrity hash—not a live Environment.

entities.title: VASTE Extension Architecture
entities.summary: Extension manifests, lifecycle, measured families, zero-extension boot, Intelligence boundaries and the current in-process trust limit.
entities.description: The implemented and specified extension system of VASTE, with a maturity-aware inventory.
entities.definition: A VASTE extension is an optional, System-scoped capability unit that declares domain actions, bindings and surfaces while runtime authority remains in the core execution path.

entities.title: The Five VASTE Primitives
entities.summary: A contract-grounded guide to the five irreducible VASTE primitives, their invariants and the concepts that must not be mistaken for primitives.
entities.description: Vertex, Tie, Action, Surface and Environment explained from VASTE contracts, validators and integration tests.
entities.definition: VASTE has a closed primitive roster—Vertex, Tie, Action, Surface and Environment—from which graph identity, topology, admitted change, observation and execution-local context are composed.

entities.title: Voice Technology
entities.summary: Voice technology spans recording, metadata, speech interfaces, voice archives, speech datasets and machine listening without reducing voice to a model output.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for voice systems inside Electronic Artefacts research.
entities.definition: Voice technology is the design and engineering of systems that capture, analyze, transform, preserve or present spoken voice as technical and cultural material.

entities.title: Web Audio
entities.summary: Web audio connects sound design, browser runtimes, interactive media, creative coding and public sound tools.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for audio systems built on the web platform.
entities.definition: Web audio is the use of browser-based audio APIs and related web technologies to synthesize, process, analyze, route and present sound.

entities.subtitle: Étude d’interaction pour Assisted Boot
entities.summary: Prototype navigateur conservé qui explore conversation VAB, mémoire modifiable, transition cérémonielle et révélation du graphe sans se substituer à l’implémentation runtime de VASTE.
entities.description: Fiche d’archive factuelle du prototype autonome HTML, CSS et JavaScript de l’interface VAB créé en juillet 2026.

entities.description: Preuves concernant primitives, exécution gouvernée, extensions, Assisted Boot et portabilité .vast, avec limites explicites.

entities.summary: Le dépôt Voice Capture Studio contient l’instrument vocal open source à cinq modes, sa constitution graphique ancrée dans le signal, ses pipelines d’observation déterministes, ses tests et son déploiement GitHub Pages.
entities.description: Fiche d’artefact pour le dépôt public Voice Capture Studio, son architecture audio locale, ses contrats de preuves et son design browser-native.

La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.

entities.title: Intelligence augmentée
entities.summary: Intelligence augmentée désigne les outils qui étendent la perception, la mémoire et le raisonnement humains sans remplacer le jugement humain. La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.
entities.description: Intelligence augmentée désigne les outils qui étendent la perception, la mémoire et le raisonnement humains sans remplacer le jugement humain. La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.
entities.definition: Intelligence augmentée désigne les outils qui étendent la perception, la mémoire et le raisonnement humains sans remplacer le jugement humain.

entities.title: Système autonome
entities.summary: Système autonome désigne les systèmes capables de poursuivre un objectif sans intervention permanente, avec des limites de contrôle explicites. La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.
entities.description: Système autonome désigne les systèmes capables de poursuivre un objectif sans intervention permanente, avec des limites de contrôle explicites. La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.
entities.definition: Système autonome désigne les systèmes capables de poursuivre un objectif sans intervention permanente, avec des limites de contrôle explicites.

entities.title: Logiciel de navigateur
entities.summary: Le logiciel de navigateur traite le navigateur comme un runtime applicatif, pas seulement comme un lecteur de documents ou une surface marketing.
entities.description: Fiche canonique pour les applications construites directement sur les capacités du navigateur.
entities.definition: Un logiciel de navigateur est une application fournie par les standards du Web et exécutée dans le runtime du navigateur pour l’interface, le stockage, les permissions, les médias et la distribution.

entities.title: Exécution contextuelle
entities.summary: L’exécution contextuelle évalue une opération à partir de l’identité des entités, des relations du graphe, des permissions, de l’état temporel et du périmètre visé.
entities.description: Un concept canonique reliant runtime de graphe, Théorie du runtime, VASTE et systèmes sensibles à l’identité.
entities.definition: L’exécution contextuelle consiste à faire de l’identité, de l’état, des permissions, des relations et du contexte opérationnel des contraintes actives sur ce qu’un système peut accomplir.

entities.title: Programmation créative
entities.summary: Programmation créative désigne l'usage du code comme médium artistique, exploratoire où pédagogique. La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.
entities.description: Programmation créative désigne l'usage du code comme médium artistique, exploratoire où pédagogique. La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.
entities.definition: Programmation créative désigne l'usage du code comme médium artistique, exploratoire où pédagogique.

entities.definition: Dans VASTE, un Actor est une identité de graphe possédée par une extension ; observation et action effectives sont résolues dans un System, un Environment, une topologie et une policy précis.

entities.title: Jeux de données vocales

entities.title: Enregistrement vocal
entities.summary: L’enregistrement vocal relie qualité audio, contexte de locuteur, prompts, transcriptions, métadonnées et révision dans un enregistrement durable.
entities.description: Fiche canonique pour les workflows professionnels de capture et de révision de parole.
entities.definition: L’enregistrement vocal est la capture dirigée de parole avec les métadonnées techniques, contextuelles et de consentement nécessaires à la révision, à la réutilisation et à la conservation.

entities.title: VAB et Genesis
entities.summary: Ce que VAB implémente, comment l’assentiment scelle un plan de boot et ce qui manque avant un handover Electronic Artefacts Genesis complet.
entities.description: Guide de VASTE Assisted Boot, du Founding Actor, de la composition et de Genesis selon leur maturité réelle.
entities.definition: VAB est le chemin d’orchestration Assisted Boot de VASTE : comprendre l’intention initiale, composer un plan scellé, admettre un Founding Actor et matérialiser un graphe initial gouverné.

entities.title: Format de graphe portable .vast
entities.summary: Modèle d’export/import .vast implémenté, garanties d’intégrité, références de frontière, round-trip testé et limites de confiance et migration.
entities.description: Guide public factuel du format expérimental de package de graphe portable VASTE.
entities.definition: Un fichier .vast est un package JSON scellé expérimental `vast/1` qui transporte une clôture relative au System — System entier ou sous-arbre — avec manifeste et hash d’intégrité, jamais un Environment vivant.

entities.title: Architecture d’extensions VASTE
entities.summary: Manifests, lifecycle, familles mesurées, boot sans extension, frontière Intelligence et limite de confiance in-process.
entities.description: Le système d’extensions implémenté et spécifié de VASTE, présenté selon sa maturité réelle.
entities.definition: Une extension VASTE est une unité optionnelle de capability, limitée à un System, qui déclare actions de domaine, bindings et Surfaces tandis que l’autorité runtime reste dans le chemin d’exécution central.

entities.title: Les cinq primitives de VASTE
entities.summary: Guide fondé sur les contrats des cinq primitives irréductibles de VASTE, de leurs invariants et des notions qui ne doivent pas être prises pour des primitives.
entities.description: Vertex, Tie, Action, Surface et Environment expliqués depuis les contrats, validateurs et tests d’intégration VASTE.
entities.definition: VASTE possède un vocabulaire fermé de cinq primitives — Vertex, Tie, Action, Surface et Environment — qui composent identité de graphe, topologie, changement admis, observation et contexte local d’exécution.

entities.title: Technologie vocale
entities.summary: La technologie vocale réunit capture, métadonnées, interfaces vocales, archives, datasets et écoute machine sans réduire la voix à une sortie de modèle.
entities.description: Fiche canonique pour les systèmes vocaux dans la recherche Electronic Artefacts.
entities.definition: La technologie vocale désigne la conception et l’ingénierie de systèmes qui capturent, analysent, transforment, préservent ou présentent la voix parlée comme matériau technique et culturel.

entities.title: Audio web
entities.summary: Audio web désigne les API audio du navigateur et les systèmes sonores interactifs produits sur le Web. La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.
entities.description: Audio web désigne les API audio du navigateur et les systèmes sonores interactifs produits sur le Web. La fiche en précise l'usage, les limites et les relations avec les projets, technologies et publications d'Electronic Artefacts.
entities.definition: Audio web désigne les API audio du navigateur et les systèmes sonores interactifs produits sur le Web.

entities.title: Electronic Artefacts Lightweight Template
entities.subtitle: Framework HTML, CSS et JavaScript léger
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts Lightweight Template est un petit framework front-end destiné à composer des sites statiques éditoriaux avec HTML, CSS et JavaScript navigateur.
entities.description: Fiche canonique du framework léger Electronic Artefacts qui compose une partie de ce site public.
entities.definition: Framework léger en HTML, CSS et JavaScript utilisé par Electronic Artefacts pour composer des prototypes, des vitrines publiques et de petites interfaces de production.

entities.title: Electronic Artefacts
entities.subtitle: Studio indépendant de technologies créatives
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts est un studio indépendant de technologies créatives qui mène des missions clients, développe ses propres systèmes et publie des travaux de recherche et de création.
entities.description: Stratégie, design, ingénierie et recherche culturelle réunis pour construire des produits numériques et des systèmes de connaissance clairs et durables.

entities.title: ORETH
entities.subtitle: Prototype archivé de recherche en synthèse vocale
entities.summary: ORETH est un prototype R&D interne archivé d’Electronic Artefacts. Il explorait l’apprentissage de la parole à partir de capteurs, sans corpus prédéfini. ORETH est aussi la signature artistique de l’album Palimpsests.
entities.description: Fiche publique distinguant le prototype archivé de synthèse vocale ORETH et sa signature artistique sur Palimpsests.

entities.title: VASTE
entities.subtitle: Recherche et implémentation d’un runtime graph-native
entities.summary: VASTE est un runtime graph-native expérimental pour construire des systèmes portables, contextuels et gouvernés à partir de cinq primitives : Vertex, Tie, Action, Surface et Environment.
entities.description: Le dossier public canonique de VASTE : architecture runtime, primitives, exécution gouvernée, extensions, Assisted Boot, format .vast expérimental, preuves et limites actuelles.

entities.title: Palimpsests
entities.subtitle: Album en cinq chapitres signé ORETH
entities.summary: Palimpsests est un album conceptuel en cinq chapitres signé ORETH. Il suit l’évolution d’un système à travers culture numérique, mémoire, transformation et transmission.
entities.description: Un dossier culturel cinématographique pour Palimpsests, album d’ORETH en cinq actes, réunissant son, image, recherche vestimentaire et matière de campagne dans un même monde visuel en évolution.

entities.title: Vestiges

entities.title: Comment une IA peut-elle comprendre quelqu'un dès sa première conversation ?
entities.subtitle: Question de recherche 001
entities.summary: Cette question étudie si l'onboarding conversationnel peut construire un premier modèle fiable d'une personne sans réduire l'intention, l'incertitude et le contexte à un formulaire statique.
entities.description: Question de recherche publique d'Electronic Artefacts sur la compréhension au premier contact, l'onboarding conversationnel et le contexte utilisateur structuré en graphe.
entities.observation: Les formulaires collectent des réponses, mais ils capturent mal l'intention, l'incertitude, le vocabulaire, le contexte évolutif et les raisons qui poussent une personne à agir maintenant.
entities.problem: Un système qui commence par des champs rigides peut confondre conformité et compréhension. Le premier échange doit préserver assez d'ambiguïté pour permettre une correction ultérieure.
entities.hypothesis: La conversation peut devenir le protocole d'onboarding si le système traite chaque échange comme une preuve, et pas seulement comme une entrée de profil.
entities.currentUnderstanding: La compréhension doit émerger progressivement. Une première conversation peut identifier des entités, objectifs, contraintes, risques et inconnues provisoires, puis garder ces hypothèses révisables.

entities.result: Le résultat le plus solide est architectural plutôt que centré sur le modèle. L'objet utile est un graphe provisoire d'hypothèses, de contraintes et de signaux inspectables.
nextSteps: Définir un schéma minimal pour les hypothèses issues de la première conversation et leurs niveaux d'incertitude.
Tester comment séparer voix, texte et métadonnées avant tout workflow de machine learning en aval.
Décider quelles hypothèses peuvent être conservées et lesquelles doivent expirer sans confirmation.

timeline.title: Cadrage de la recherche
timeline.summary: La question a été séparée de l'onboarding IA générique et reformulée autour de la compréhension au premier contact.

timeline.title: Intégration dans l'atlas de la page d'accueil
timeline.summary: La question devient une fiche du graphe reliée aux logiciels et preuves actuels.

entities.title: Comment un logiciel peut-il devenir explicable par son propre graphe de connaissance ?
entities.subtitle: Question de recherche 003
entities.summary: Cette question étudie si un logiciel peut expliquer son objectif, ses preuves, dépendances et limites à travers le même graphe qui génère ses pages publiques et ses fiches machine-readable.
entities.description: Question de recherche publique d'Electronic Artefacts sur les logiciels explicables, les graphes de connaissance, la documentation générée et les surfaces publiques sémantiques.
entities.observation: La documentation devient vite obsolète lorsqu'elle est séparée des entités, relations et routes que le système logiciel utilise réellement.
entities.problem: Un projet peut avoir pages, JSON-LD, données de recherche, exports de relations et fichiers source qui divergent si aucun graphe structuré ne joue le rôle de couche d'autorité.
entities.hypothesis: Le graphe de connaissance peut devenir la couche documentaire principale lorsque pages, identifiants, voisinages de graphe, documents de recherche et panneaux relationnels sont générés depuis les mêmes fiches source.
entities.currentUnderstanding: L'explicabilité progresse lorsque l'interface publique expose le graphe au lieu de le cacher. Une page devrait montrer ce qu'elle est, ses liens, ses preuves et les limites de ses affirmations.

experiments.title: Pages d'entités générées
experiments.summary: Le site Electronic Artefacts génère pages canoniques, identifiants, JSON-LD, documents de recherche et voisinages de graphe depuis des fiches Markdown typées.

experiments.title: Panneaux relationnels
experiments.summary: Les pages de projets, programmes, concepts et publications exposent des relations typées pour expliquer pourquoi les fiches sont connectées.

entities.result: Le site démontre déjà une réponse partielle. HTML public, identifiants, JSON-LD, recherche et fichiers de graphe local sont générés depuis des fiches typées plutôt que maintenus séparément.
nextSteps: Ajouter davantage de relations dérivées tout en conservant la revue éditoriale.
Rendre les voisinages de graphe plus faciles à inspecter depuis les pages publiques.
Définir ce qui constitue une preuve suffisante avant qu'une relation devienne canonique.

timeline.title: Requalification de la documentation
timeline.summary: L'architecture du site est reformulée comme graphe public plutôt que comme suite de pages de portfolio maintenues à la main.

timeline.title: Ajout de l'entité question de recherche
timeline.summary: Les questions de recherche deviennent des entités du graphe, permettant aux projets et logiciels de pointer vers ce qu'ils testent.

entities.title: Comment les datasets vocaux peuvent-ils devenir reproductibles, structurés et privacy-first ?
entities.subtitle: Question de recherche 002
entities.summary: Cette question étudie comment l'enregistrement vocal dans le navigateur peut standardiser acquisition, métadonnées et revue tout en gardant la matière vocale privée sous contrôle local.
entities.description: Question de recherche publique d'Electronic Artefacts sur les datasets vocaux reproductibles, l'enregistrement local-first et les workflows vocaux riches en métadonnées.
entities.observation: Les datasets vocaux sont souvent assemblés avec des outils hétérogènes, des dossiers libres et des métadonnées manquantes, ce qui rend la revue, le consentement et la reproduction difficiles.
entities.problem: La qualité du dataset est souvent discutée après l'enregistrement, lorsque de nombreuses conditions importantes ont déjà disparu. La confidentialité devient aussi plus difficile à vérifier si l'outil masque le lieu de stockage de l'audio.
entities.hypothesis: Un workflow d'enregistrement dans le navigateur peut standardiser acquisition, états de revue, métadonnées et structure d'export sans logiciel propriétaire ni upload distant.
entities.currentUnderstanding: Capturer les données est aussi important qu'entraîner les modèles. Un workflow vocal utile doit préserver prompt, locuteur, timing, qualité, revue et contexte d'export avant tout travail de modèle.

experiments.title: Workspace local dans le navigateur
experiments.summary: Voice Capture Studio garde les enregistrements dans le stockage local du navigateur ou dans des dossiers explicitement choisis, puis exporte une session structurée.

experiments.title: Export WAV et métadonnées
experiments.summary: Le projet teste si audio, transcription, timing, qualité et manifestes peuvent former une seule sortie de session reproductible.

entities.result: La réponse logicielle immédiate est Voice Capture Studio : un enregistreur local-first dans le navigateur avec exports structurés et séparation explicite entre enregistrement et entraînement.
nextSteps: Étendre les définitions de corpus en gardant source, version et langue explicites.
Tester les exports avec des workflows de revue et d'alignement en aval.
Définir des notes de consentement et de suppression qui restent attachées aux manifestes.

entities.title: How can an AI understand someone during its very first conversation?
entities.subtitle: Research Question 001
entities.summary: This research question studies whether conversational onboarding can build a reliable first model of a person without reducing intent, uncertainty and context to a static form.
entities.description: A public Electronic Artefacts research question about first-contact understanding, conversational onboarding and graph-shaped user context.
entities.observation: Forms collect answers, but they often fail to capture intent, uncertainty, vocabulary, changing context and the reasons a person gives for acting now.
entities.problem: A system that starts from rigid fields can mistake compliance for understanding. The first interaction should preserve enough ambiguity for later correction instead of forcing premature classification.
entities.hypothesis: Conversation itself can become the onboarding protocol when the system treats each exchange as evidence, not merely as input for a profile form.
entities.currentUnderstanding: Understanding should emerge progressively. A first conversation can identify provisional entities, goals, constraints, risks and unknowns, then keep those claims revisable as more evidence arrives.

experiments.title: Assisted Boot interface prototype
experiments.summary: A preserved autonomous VAB browser prototype explores provisional understanding, editable memory projection, user confirmation and graph reveal while explicitly separating interface simulation from canonical runtime state.

experiments.title: Conversational brief capture
experiments.summary: Contact and discovery flows are treated as structured conversations that identify intent, pathway, missing context and useful next questions.

experiments.title: Voice context capture
experiments.summary: Voice Capture Studio tests how speech capture can preserve transcript, timing, speaker and quality metadata without sending recordings to a remote service.

entities.result: The strongest result so far is architectural rather than model-centric. The useful object is a provisional graph of claims, constraints and signals that can be inspected and revised.
nextSteps: Define a minimal schema for first-conversation claims and uncertainty levels.
Test how voice, text and metadata should be separated before downstream machine-learning work.
Decide which claims are safe to persist and which should expire unless confirmed.

timeline.title: Research framing
timeline.summary: The question was separated from generic AI onboarding and reframed around first-contact understanding.

timeline.title: Homepage atlas integration
timeline.summary: The question became a first-class knowledge-graph record connected to current software and evidence.

entities.title: How can software become explainable through its own knowledge graph?
entities.subtitle: Research Question 003
entities.summary: This research question studies whether software can explain its purpose, evidence, dependencies and limits through the same graph that generates its public pages and machine-readable records.
entities.description: A public Electronic Artefacts research question about self-explaining software, knowledge graphs, generated documentation and semantic public surfaces.
entities.observation: Documentation quickly becomes outdated when it is separate from the entities, relations and routes that a software system actually uses.
entities.problem: A project can have pages, JSON-LD, search data, relation exports and source files that disagree unless one structured graph acts as the authority layer.
entities.hypothesis: The knowledge graph itself can become the primary documentation layer when pages, identifiers, graph neighborhoods, search documents and relation panels are generated from the same source records.
entities.currentUnderstanding: Explainability improves when the public interface exposes the graph instead of hiding it. A page should show what it is, what it connects to, what evidence supports it and where its claims remain limited.

experiments.title: Generated entity pages
experiments.summary: The Electronic Artefacts site generates canonical pages, identifiers, JSON-LD, search documents and graph neighborhoods from typed Markdown records.

experiments.title: Relationship panels
experiments.summary: Project, program, concept and publication pages expose typed relations so readers can inspect why records are connected.

entities.result: The site already demonstrates a partial answer. Its public HTML, identifiers, JSON-LD, search documents and local graph files are generated from typed records rather than maintained as separate explanations.
nextSteps: Add more derived relation patterns while preserving editorial review.
Make graph neighborhoods easier to inspect from public pages.
Define what counts as enough evidence before a relation becomes canonical.

timeline.title: Documentation reframing
timeline.summary: The site architecture was reframed as a public graph rather than a set of manually maintained portfolio pages.

timeline.title: Research-question entity added
timeline.summary: Research questions became graph entities, allowing projects and software to point back to the questions they test.

entities.title: How can speech datasets become reproducible, structured and privacy-first?
entities.subtitle: Research Question 002
entities.summary: This research question studies how browser-based speech recording can standardize acquisition, metadata and review while keeping private voice material under local control.
entities.description: A public Electronic Artefacts research question about reproducible speech datasets, local-first recording and metadata-rich voice workflows.
entities.observation: Voice datasets are often assembled through inconsistent tools, loose folders and missing metadata, which makes later review, consent checking and reproduction difficult.
entities.problem: Dataset quality is frequently discussed after recording, when many important conditions have already been lost. Privacy also becomes harder to reason about when capture tools hide where audio is stored.
entities.hypothesis: A browser-based recording workflow can standardize acquisition, review states, metadata and export structure without requiring proprietary desktop software or remote upload.
entities.currentUnderstanding: Capturing data is as important as training models. A useful speech workflow should preserve prompt, speaker, timing, quality, review and export context before any downstream model work begins.

experiments.title: Local browser workspace
experiments.summary: Voice Capture Studio keeps recordings in browser-local storage or explicit user-selected folders, then exports structured session material.

experiments.title: WAV and metadata export
experiments.summary: The project tests whether audio, transcript, timing, quality and manifest files can be treated as one reproducible capture-session output.

entities.result: The immediate software answer is Voice Capture Studio: a local-first browser recorder with structured export boundaries and explicit separation between recording and model training.
nextSteps: Expand corpus definitions while keeping source, version and language context explicit.
Test export packages against downstream review and alignment workflows.
Define consent and deletion notes that remain attached to dataset manifests.

timeline.title: Dataset workflow split
timeline.summary: The research separated speech capture from downstream model training so the capture boundary could be designed directly.

timeline.title: Open-source project publication
timeline.summary: Voice Capture Studio became the public software answer and evidence source for the question.

timeline.title: Six-part editorial evidence layer
timeline.summary: Six bilingual publications connected interaction, capture provenance, deterministic evidence, segmentation, product modes and browser architecture back to the research question.
